# 🖼️ How to Add Your Project Images

## Folder Structure

This is where you put your project images:

```
projects/
├── branding/          ← Brand identity, logos, packaging
├── social-media/      ← Instagram, Facebook, Twitter graphics
├── print-design/      ← Flyers, posters, brochures, menus
├── digital-design/    ← UI/UX, app designs, websites
└── thumbnails/        ← YouTube thumbnails, video graphics
```

## Steps to Add Your Images

### 1. Copy Your Images Here

Drag and drop your images into the appropriate folder based on the project category.

**Example:**
```
projects/
└── print-design/
    ├── church-flyer-main.jpg
    ├── church-flyer-back.jpg
    └── church-flyer-mockup.jpg
```

### 2. Update the Project Data

Open: `app/data/projects.ts`

Find the section that says "ADD YOUR NEW PROJECTS BELOW THIS LINE" and add your project:

```javascript
{
  id: "9",
  slug: "church-event-flyer",
  title: "Church Event Flyer",
  category: "Print Design",
  thumbnail: "/projects/print-design/church-flyer-main.jpg",
  images: [
    "/projects/print-design/church-flyer-main.jpg",
    "/projects/print-design/church-flyer-back.jpg",
    "/projects/print-design/church-flyer-mockup.jpg"
  ],
  problem: "Describe the challenge",
  solution: "Explain your solution",
  process: [
    "Your design process steps"
  ],
  results: "The outcome",
  year: "2024",
  client: "Client Name"
}
```

### 3. Save and Refresh

- Save the `projects.ts` file
- The dev server will automatically reload
- Refresh your browser to see the new project

## Image Guidelines

### Recommended Sizes:
- **Thumbnails:** 800×600px (shown in grid)
- **Detail Images:** 1200×800px or larger (shown in project page)

### File Formats:
- JPG (best for photos)
- PNG (best for graphics with transparency)
- WebP (best for web, smaller file size)

### File Naming:
Use descriptive, URL-friendly names:
- ✅ `church-flyer-main.jpg`
- ✅ `coffee-logo-final.png`
- ❌ `IMG_1234.jpg`
- ❌ `Final Version 2 (1).png`

### Keep Files Small:
- Compress images before uploading
- Aim for under 500KB per image
- Use tools like TinyPNG.com or Squoosh.app

## Quick Tips

### To Replace ALL Images:
1. Delete placeholder projects in `app/data/projects.ts`
2. Add your own projects with your image paths
3. Update categories in `app/data/categories.ts` if needed

### To Replace Just ONE Image:
1. Find the project in `app/data/projects.ts`
2. Change the URL to your new image path
3. Save and refresh

### To Add More Images to a Project:
Just add more URLs to the `images` array:
```javascript
images: [
  "/projects/print-design/image1.jpg",
  "/projects/print-design/image2.jpg",
  "/projects/print-design/image3.jpg",
  "/projects/print-design/image4.jpg",  // Add as many as you want!
]
```

## Need Help?

Check the example project template in `app/data/projects.ts` - it shows you exactly what fields to fill in!
